---
title: "MCQ Exercises"
author: Amanda Ng
format: 
  html:
    css: [webex.css]
    include-after-body: [webex.js]
    embed-resources: true 
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, results='asis'}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)

library(webexercises)
```

## Numpy 

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}

```{r, results='asis'}
opts <- c(
  "`np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6], shape=(2,3))`",
  answer = "`np.array([[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]])`",
  "`np.array((1, 2, 3), (4, 5, 6))`",
  "`np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4], [5, 6]])`"
)

cat("Q1. Which of the following correctly creates a 2×3 NumPy array?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis'}
opts <- c(
  "An array of integers from 0 to 10",
  answer = "An array of 5 evenly spaced values from 0 to 10",
  "An array of values from 0 to 10 with step size 5",
  "An array of 10 evenly spaced values from 0 to 5"
)

cat("Q2. What does `np.linspace(0, 10, 5)` produce?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis'}
opts <- c(
  "(3, 2)",
  answer = "(2, 3)",
  "(6,)",
  "(1, 6)"
)

cat("Q3. If `x = np.array([[1,2,3],[4,5,6]])`, what is `x.shape`?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "2",
  answer = "2.5",
  "[2.5]",
  "It raises an error"
)

cat("Q4. Given `a = np.array([1,2,3,4])`, what does `a.mean()` return?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  answer = "np.sqrt()",
  "np.square()",
  "np.exp()",
  "np.root()"
)

cat("Q5. Which NumPy function computes the square root of every element in an array?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "Converts it into a 3×2 array",
  answer = "Flattens it into a 1D array of length 6",
  "Creates a copy with reversed rows",
  "Raises an error"
)

cat("Q6. What does `x.reshape(6,)` do to a 2×3 array?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "Both return views of the original array",
  "Both return copies of the original array",
  answer = "`flatten()` returns a copy; `ravel()` returns a view",
  "`flatten()` returns a view; `ravel()` returns a copy"
)

cat("Q7. Which statement about `flatten()` and `ravel()` is correct?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "[10, 40]",
  answer = "[20, 50]",
  "[30, 60]",
  "[[20], [50]]"
)

cat("Q8. Given `x = np.array([[10,20,30],[40,50,60]])`, what does `x[:,1]` return?", longmcq(opts))
```

:::

## Pandas 

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "A Series must contain only numeric values",
  answer = "A Series represents a single column of data",
  "A Series is always two‑dimensional",
  "A Series can only be created from a dictionary"
)

cat("Q1. Which statement correctly describes a Pandas Series?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "`df.head(10)`",
  "`df.show(5)`",
  answer = "`df.tail(5)`",
  "`df.last(5)`"
)

cat("Q2. Which command displays the last 5 rows of a DataFrame `df`?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "`df.remove_na()`",
  answer = "`df.dropna()`",
  "`df.delete_na()`",
  "`df.clean()`"
)

cat("Q3. Which function removes all rows containing at least one NA value?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "`df(Age, Gender)`",
  "`df.select([\"Age\",\"Gender\"])`",
  answer = "`df[[\"Age\",\"Gender\"]]`",
  "`df[\"Age\",\"Gender\"]`"
)

cat("Q4. Which command correctly extracts the columns 'Age' and 'Gender' as a DataFrame?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "`df[df.Age > 40 & df.Heart_attack == \"No\"]`",
  "`df[(Age > 40) and (Heart_attack == \"No\")]`",
  answer = "`df[(df[\"Age\"] > 40) & (df[\"Heart_attack\"] == \"No\")]`",
  "`df.filter(Age > 40 & Heart_attack == \"No\")`"
)

cat("Q5. Which command correctly filters rows where age is over 40 and has not experienced heart attack?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "`df.age_5_years_later = Age + 5`",
  "`df[\"age_5_years_later\"] == df[\"Age\"] + 5`",
  answer = "`df[\"age_5_years_later\"] = df[\"Age\"] + 5`",
  "`df.new(age_5_years_later = Age + 5)`"
)

cat("Q6. Which command correctly creates a new column storing age 5 years later?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "`df[\"gender_text\"] = df[\"Gender\"].ifelse(\"Male\",\"Female\")`",
  "`df[\"gender_text\"] = df[\"Gender\"].where(df[\"Gender\"]==1,\"Male\",\"Female\")`",
  answer = "`df[\"gender_text\"] = np.where(df[\"Gender\"]==1, \"Male\", \"Female\")`",
  "`df[\"gender_text\"] = Gender == 1 ? \"Male\" : \"Female\"`"
)

cat("Q7. Which command correctly assigns 'Male' when Gender==1 and 'Female' otherwise?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "`df.groupby(\"BMI\")[\"Heart_attack\"].mean()`",
  "`df.group(\"Heart_attack\").BMI.mean()`",
  answer = "`df.groupby(\"Heart_attack\")[[\"BMI\"]].mean()`",
  "`df[\"BMI\"].groupby(\"Heart_attack\").summary()`"
)

cat("Q8. Which command computes mean BMI grouped by Heart_attack status?", longmcq(opts))
```
:::

## Scikitlearn 

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "`sklearn.preprocessing`",
  answer = "`sklearn.linear_model`",
  "`sklearn.metrics`",
  "`sklearn.model_selection`"
)

cat("Q1. From which module do you import `LinearRegression`?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "It accepts text labels directly with no changes",
  answer = "Binary outcomes must be encoded as 0/1 values",
  "It requires outcomes to be integers from 1 to 10",
  "It automatically converts strings to numeric codes"
)

cat("Q2. Which statement about preparing the outcome for logistic regression is correct?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "It trains the model on the entire dataset",
  "It always uses a 50:50 split",
  answer = "It separates data into training and testing sets for model evaluation",
  "It only works for classification models"
)

cat("Q3. What is the purpose of `train_test_split()`?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "Both methods eliminate predictors completely",
  answer = "LASSO can shrink some coefficients to zero, while Ridge generally does not",
  "Ridge always performs better than LASSO",
  "LASSO and Ridge produce identical models"
)

cat("Q4. Which statement correctly compares LASSO and Ridge regression?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "KNN estimates coefficients like linear regression",
  "KNN requires predictors to be categorical",
  answer = "KNN predicts based on the labels of the k closest training samples",
  "KNN cannot be used for classification"
)

cat("Q5. Which statement correctly describes how KNN makes predictions?", longmcq(opts))
```
:::

## Matplotlib 
::: {.webex-check .webex-box}

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "An Axes is the entire window that contains multiple plots",
  answer = "A Figure is the overall container; an Axes is the area where data is plotted",
  "A Figure and an Axes are the same object",
  "Axes objects can exist without being inside a Figure"
)

cat("Q1. Which statement correctly describes the relationship between a Figure and an Axes in Matplotlib?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "`plt.figure(2,2)` creates a 2×2 grid of subplots",
  "`plt.plot()` automatically creates a 2×2 layout",
  answer = "`plt.subplots(2, 2)` creates a Figure with a 2×2 grid of Axes",
  "You must manually add each Axes using `add_axes()`"
)

cat("Q2. Which command correctly creates a 2×2 grid of subplots?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "`ax.line(x, y)`",
  "`plt.scatter(ax, x, y)`",
  answer = "`ax.scatter(x, y)`",
  "`fig.scatter(x, y)`"
)

cat("Q3. Which command correctly creates a scatterplot on an Axes object?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "`ax.hist(df[\"Age\"], bins=\"auto\", color=\"none\")`",
  answer = "`ax.hist(df[\"Age\"], bins=10, edgecolor=\"black\")`",
  "`ax.hist(df[\"Age\"], breaks=10)`",
  "`ax.histogram(df[\"Age\"], bins=10)`"
)

cat("Q4. Which command correctly creates a histogram of Age with 10 bins and visible bin edges?", longmcq(opts))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  "The box shows the mean and standard deviation",
  answer = "The box spans Q1 to Q3, with a line at the median",
  "The whiskers always extend to the minimum and maximum values",
  "Boxplots cannot display means"
)

cat("Q5. Which statement correctly describes the components of a Matplotlib boxplot?", longmcq(opts))
```

:::

## Return to Learning Hub Homepage

[Learning Hub Homepage](https://uoft-hmb-coding.github.io){.btn .btn-primary}
